title: 2D Arrays & Traversal - Coding Quiz
description: Write Python programs that work with 2D lists (grids), using nested loops for traversal and performing operations on rows, columns, and diagonals.
subject: Programming

questionText:
Write a program that reads a 2D grid and prints the sum of all elements.

First read two integers: R (rows) and C (columns) on one line separated by a space.
Then read R lines, each containing C space-separated integers.
Print: Total: <sum>
subject: Programming

content.starterCode:
r, c = map(int, input().split())
grid = []
for i in range(r):
    row = list(map(int, input().split()))
    grid.append(row)

# Calculate and print total sum
content.explanation: Use nested loops to visit every element and add it to a running total. Or sum each row and add the row sums.

questionText:
Write a program that reads a square grid (NxN) and prints the main diagonal elements.

Read integer N, then N rows of N space-separated integers.
Print the diagonal elements separated by spaces on one line.
subject: Programming

content.starterCode:
n = int(input())
grid = []
for i in range(n):
    row = list(map(int, input().split()))
    grid.append(row)

# Print main diagonal elements
content.explanation: The main diagonal is where row == col. Loop i from 0 to n-1 and collect grid[i][i]. Use print(*diag) or ' '.join().

questionText:
Write a program that reads a grid and prints the sum of each row.

Read R and C on one line (space-separated), then R rows of C space-separated integers.
For each row, print: Row <number>: <sum>
(Row numbers start at 1)
subject: Programming

content.starterCode:
r, c = map(int, input().split())
grid = []
for i in range(r):
    row = list(map(int, input().split()))
    grid.append(row)

# Print sum of each row
content.explanation: Loop through each row with an index. Use sum(grid[i]) to get the row total and print it.

questionText:
Write a program that reads a grid and finds the largest element along with its position.

Read R and C on one line, then the grid.
Print: Max: <value> at row <r> col <c>
(Use 0-based row and column indices)
subject: Programming

content.starterCode:
r, c = map(int, input().split())
grid = []
for i in range(r):
    row = list(map(int, input().split()))
    grid.append(row)

# Find maximum element and its position
content.explanation: Track the max value, max_row, and max_col as you traverse. Update whenever grid[r][c] > current max.

questionText:
Write a program that reads a square grid and prints its transpose (rows become columns, columns become rows).

Read integer N, then the NxN grid. Print the transposed grid, with each row on its own line and values separated by spaces.
subject: Programming

content.starterCode:
n = int(input())
grid = []
for i in range(n):
    row = list(map(int, input().split()))
    grid.append(row)

# Print transposed grid (rows become columns)
content.explanation: In a transpose, element at [r][c] moves to [c][r]. Loop column first (outer), then row (inner), printing grid[row][col].
